title: plagiobothrys fulvus
intro: Plagiobothrys fulvus, also known as Yellow Fiddleneck, is a flowering plant species in the borage family that is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Yellow Fiddleneck include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Yellow Fiddleneck can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
